what_it_does: Renders a layered task-tree visualization called 'Vi' with a TaskNode component for individual nodes and a TaskTree component that maps an array of layers into stacked layer panels separated by connectors.
observations: Two React functional components: TaskNode and TaskTree.
TaskNode renders a name, optional meta text, and a CSS class derived directly from the state prop.
TaskTree iterates over a layers prop, rendering a header (tag + description), a list of TaskNode components, and a connector between consecutive layers.
The component is exposed globally via window.TaskTree rather than exported.
No imports, prop-types, or default exports are present; React and window are assumed to be globally available.
Array indices are used as React keys.
standouts: Global window.TaskTree registration instead of ES module export.
State string is interpolated directly into a CSS class name.
Explicit three-layer visual structure with inter-layer connectors.
conventions: Global namespace attachment for reusable UI-kit components.
concerns: state prop is concatenated into className without validation or sanitization.
No prop-type or TypeScript shape definition for layers/nodes.
Relies on global React and window, which may break in strict module environments.
Index keys can cause rendering issues if layer/node order changes.
No accessibility markup (roles, labels) for the tree visualization.
notes: A small, global-registered React view component for Vi's layered agent task tree, useful as a UI-kit widget but tightly coupled to a global React runtime.
questions: What is the exact schema of the layers and nodes objects expected by TaskTree?
Where are the corresponding .vi-* CSS rules defined?
Is React guaranteed to be globally available at runtime?
Why is the component attached to window instead of being exported as a module?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose ('Vi's three-layer agent visualisation') matches the implementation, which renders named layers of task nodes connected visually.
intention_signals: Provide a lightweight, reusable React widget that visualizes an agent's task structure as a stack of named layers containing status-coded nodes.
novelty: The explicit connector-div between layers and the global window assignment for a design-system UI kit component.
contribution: A compact layered-tree React component pattern that separates node rendering from layer layout and exposes a global registration hook for integration into a larger UI kit.
summary_for_embedding: TaskTree.jsx is a global-registered React UI-kit component that renders Vi's three-layer agent task visualization using TaskNode items connected by layer separators.
intention_for_embedding: This file was intended to provide a reusable, presentational React widget for displaying layered agent task trees in the Vi design system.

symbols.does: Renders a single task node with a status dot, name, optional meta text, and a CSS class based on state.
symbols.description: TaskNode is a presentational React component that takes name, meta, and state props and returns a div with top-level status styling, a dot indicator, the node name, and an optional metadata line.

symbols.does: Maps a layers array into stacked layer panels containing TaskNode lists, with connectors between layers.
symbols.description: TaskTree is a presentational React component that receives a layers prop, renders each layer with a numbered tag and description, maps its nodes to TaskNode components, and inserts a visual connector between consecutive layers; it is finally assigned to window.TaskTree.
